explanation_box.text: The white stripe on its belly means it is well hydrated.
The green stripe on its neck indicates malnourishment.
The red coloring indicates algae poisoning.
The clear tan color on its chin and belly stripe indicate healthy reproductive function.
The pointy toes indicate frequent stressors in its environment.
The round, evenly colored toes indicate low stress, and the healthy white stripe on its belly indicates good hydration.
Although the pointed toes indicate stress, the bright green coloring and clear belly stripes indicate good overall health otherwise.
The round toes indicate low stress, the clear tan belly stripe indicates good hydration, and the tan chin shows adequate nutrition.
The pointed toes are a sign of stress, which this one must have a low tolerance for.
The belly stripe and tan chin show good hydration and nutritional intake, and the rounded toes show low stress.
The pointed toes and red coloring indicate stress and algal contamination, only slightly offset by healthy nutritional intake indicated by the tan chin.
This animal must have a low stress tolerance threshold, since its toes are pointed, indicating a high-stress environment, but its other markers are good.
Although this specimen has rounded toes and good belly stripe and chin coloring, The darkness of the neck band indicates mate aggression.
This specimen has a bright belly stripe and chin patch, as well as rounded toes, offsetting its algal contamination sufficiently to increase fertility levels.
Although this specimen has a bright chin patch, the pointed toes indicate a stressful environment, and the faded belly stripe indicates poor hydration.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?
